title: Joy in writing for the Inquirer
sub_topics: Salonga's advice against retired-CJ column writing
FEU Advocate and FEU Law Review student-journalism background
carpal tunnel from years of writing
'With Due Respect' and 'With Due Respect 2' column compilations
Information Age tools (Google, iPhone 6-plus, iMessage, Telegram, Signal, Viber)

stances.claim: Writing a weekly Inquirer column for ten years without fail — through illness, carpal tunnel, business commitments, speaking engagements, and travel abroad — has been one of the ringing joys of his life and an act of intellectual continuity post-retirement.
stances.rhetorical_move: anniversary reflection + consistency claim as discipline-credential

stances.claim: He enjoys writing for the Inquirer because of complete editorial freedom on any subject of his choice (mirroring the Supreme Court freedom 'guided only by prayer, the Constitution, the law, and ultimately, by what is just and fair') and because column writing forces him to stay updated on the latest news.
stances.rhetorical_move: parallel framing — Inquirer freedom mirrors SC opinion-writing freedom

stances.claim: At his advanced age, he has no more ambition for himself or his family — by writing his opinion, he can contribute his 'humble thoughts to the molding of unfettered policy and polity.'

He had to pivot from analog to digital, from Nokia to iPhone 6-plus, to keep up with 'the exponential growth of knowledge' — column writing required adopting newer research methods and AI-aware tools.

events: Publication of 'With Due Respect' (2011) and 'With Due Respect 2' (2017)

signature_phrases: One of the ringing joys of my life
from hawking newspapers in the backstreets of Sampaloc
chief justices make history, they do not write it
the molding of unfettered policy and polity
I had to pivot from analog to digital technology
decision_framework_signals: applies discipline-of-continuity principle (weekly column for 10 years without fail) as habit-formation device
frames opinion writing as civic-contribution mechanism in post-ambition stage of life
uses technological-adaptation narrative to demonstrate continued learning at advanced age
cross_references: the very recent publication of a brand-new book compiling my 2011-2016 columns, titled 'With Due Respect 2'
notable_anecdotes: Salonga's advice against the column: 'chief justices make history, they do not write it. I do not know of any retired chief justice who had penned a column'
Marixi Prieto requesting 3 columns/week, CJ negotiating down to 1
Writing his FEU Advocate and FEU Law Review pieces as the only prior journalism background — 'absolutely no background in opinion writing'
Authoring 1,200+ full SC decisions plus 'one book a year' during 11 years on the Court, leaving him wanting to rest in retirement
